steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens an inserted phrase after "drastically" with an em dash: "cores now number in the dozens, hundreds, or even thousands." The blank must close that inserted phrase after "thousands" so the larger so ... that sentence can continue.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘thousands, that’: A comma cannot close the inserted dash phrase. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘thousands that—’: Places the dash after "that," which leaves the inserted phrase unclosed. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘thousands—that’: The em dash closes the inserted phrase, and "that" then completes the so drastically ... that construction. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘thousands. That’: Splits the result clause into a fragment. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
